ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for bánh mì or Vietnamese baguette sandwich. FOODON_00002473 (sandwich food product) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKP (Sandwiches) is the closest parent category. No specific bánh mì code exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226559009 (Sandwich) is the closest available parent concept. No specific bánh mì concept exists in SNOMED CT.

main_ingredients: Vietnamese pork roll (cha lua / chả lụa)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard bánh mì baguette sandwich weighing approximately 200 g, comprising a French-style baguette roll filled with chả lụa (steamed pork roll), pork liver pâté, pickled vegetables, cucumber, coriander, and condiments.

nutrient_source.title: Bánh mì (Vietnamese sandwich) — composite nutrient estimation based on USDA FoodData Central ingredient data: French bread (FDC ID 325871), pork liver pâté (FDC ID 167904), pork luncheon meat / cooked pork roll (FDC ID 174609), pickled vegetables, cucumber, and condiments
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (Foundation Foods / SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact bánh mì entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data for French baguette (~55% by weight), chả lụa / cooked pork roll (~20%), pork liver pâté (~8%), pickled daikon-carrot (~7%), cucumber (~5%), and condiments/herbs (~5%). Values are per 100 g of the assembled sandwich. Confidence is medium.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; white bread / French baguette reference (GI ~72, glucose scale)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for bánh mì as a composite dish. The GI of 72 is derived from the dominant carbohydrate source: French baguette / white wheat bread, which has a well-established GI of approximately 72 (glucose scale) per the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283). The pâté, pork roll, and vegetable fillings contribute negligible additional glycemic load. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (72 × 55 g carbohydrate in 200 g serving) / 100 = 39.6.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~620 mg per 100 g; ~1240 mg per sandwich serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid bánh mì pâté chả lụa due to its high sodium load from pork liver pâté, chả lụa, soy sauce, and Maggi seasoning, which can contribute over 50% of the recommended daily sodium intake of 2000 mg in a single serving.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (GI 72) and high glycemic load (~39.6 per serving) from refined white baguette
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume bánh mì pâté chả lụa with caution, as the refined white baguette base produces a high glycemic load (~39.6 per 200 g serving), which can cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation; portion reduction or substitution with a wholegrain roll is advisable.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat (~2.4 g per 100 g) and high sodium from processed pork products and condiments
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals with cardiovascular disease, the combination of saturated fat from pork liver pâté and chả lụa and the high sodium content warrants portion control and infrequent consumption, as regular intake may adversely affect blood pressure and lipid profiles.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Bánh mì pâté chả lụa is a Vietnamese-style baguette sandwich that has become a popular street food and café item across Southeast Asia, including Singapore. The sandwich is built on a light, crispy French-style baguette — a legacy of French colonial influence in Vietnam — and filled with a combination of pork liver pâté, chả lụa (Vietnamese steamed pork roll seasoned with fish sauce and black pepper), pickled daikon and carrot (đồ chua), fresh cucumber slices, spring onion, coriander, sliced chilli, and a spread of butter or mayonnaise, often finished with Maggi seasoning or soy sauce. The result is a complex interplay of savoury, tangy, herbaceous, and mildly spicy flavours with contrasting textures — crisp crust, tender crumb, silky pâté, and crunchy pickled vegetables. Nutritionally, the sandwich is a moderate-to-high calorie food (~218 kcal per 100 g) with a meaningful protein contribution (~9.8 g per 100 g) from the pork-based fillings. The dominant carbohydrate source is the refined white baguette, which drives a high glycemic index of approximately 72 and a high glycemic load per serving (~39.6), making portion control important for individuals managing blood glucose. Sodium content is elevated (~620 mg per 100 g) due to the processed pork products and condiments, which is a concern for hypertension and cardiovascular disease management. The dish provides iron, B vitamins, and vitamin A from the pâté and pork roll components. In Singapore, bánh mì is commonly served at Vietnamese restaurants and food stalls, and is classified as a mixed-cuisine street food item.

confidence_reason: No exact bánh mì pâté chả lụa entry was found in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data for the constituent ingredients (French baguette, pork liver pâté, chả lụa, pickled vegetables, condiments) weighted by approximate proportions in a standard serving. GI is estimated from the French baguette component using the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium due to the composite estimation approach.
notes: Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central source data for the component ingredients at the level of precision available for this composite estimation. All other -1 fields have been replaced with estimated values where component data permitted. Nutrient values are per 100 g of the assembled sandwich. Serving size of 200 g is based on a typical single bánh mì baguette sandwich portion. The food is classified under Singapore cuisine in the input record but is of Vietnamese origin; the cuisine field has been updated to Vietnamese to reflect culinary origin, consistent with the restaurant source (Thien Long Vietnam Restaurant). The food is not halal-certified as it contains pork products. GI of 72 is an analogous estimate based on the French baguette component per University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008.
